qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Chromium URL conversion fetches internal URLs and returns their content
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a separate internal-HTTP SSRF issue. Its evidence and PoC rely on default allowance of internal HTTP targets and explicitly do not identify the advisory's mixed-case file-scheme deny-list bypass in Chromium filtering.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Concurrent downloadFrom handling crashes Gotenberg with concurrent map writes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a denial-of-service bug in pkg/modules/api/context.go caused by unsynchronized map writes in downloadFrom handling, not a Chromium deny-list bypass or mixed-case file-scheme filtering flaw.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ExifTool metadata write moves output files outside the request cleanup directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an ExifTool metadata-to-filesystem boundary issue that moves output PDFs outside the request workdir; it does not involve Chromium URL filtering, the default file deny-list, or the advisory's case-insensitive file-scheme bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: a mixed-case or uppercase file-scheme bypass of the Chromium default deny-list caused by case-sensitive regex matching in the URL filtering path.
evidence: The advisory describes FILE:/File: URLs bypassing the default ^file:(?!//\\/tmp/).* deny-list because pkg/gotenberg/filter.go recompiles regexes with case-sensitive flags before Chromium normalizes the scheme.

The session's Chromium qualifying finding is about internal HTTP SSRF with PDF exfiltration; its PoC evidence uses http://host.docker.internal:8123/page-1-html/index.html and does not exercise a mixed-case file: URL.

artifacts/component_family_checklist.md states for the Chromium family that direct file:// reads outside /tmp were treated as blocked by the default deny list, which is inconsistent with the advisory's actual vulnerable condition.
The other qualifying findings are a downloadFrom concurrent map write crash and an ExifTool metadata file-move primitive, both in different components and with different root causes.
